decision: Keep 2 Vercel projects (quiz + admin), delete 3 duplicates
reasoning: 3 duplicate projects waste edge requests and create confusion
expected_outcome: Cleaner infra, less confusion, lower costs

decision: Use parameterized queries for all SQL in admin dashboard
reasoning: Raw SQL concatenation was creating SQL injection vulnerabilities
expected_outcome: Zero SQL injection vectors in admin API routes

decision: Add rate limiting + CSRF protection to admin API
reasoning: API routes had no abuse protection
expected_outcome: Prevent brute force and CSRF attacks on admin

decision: Landing page redesign: Canvas/WebGL particles for hero animation
reasoning: User wants premium Apple+Disney experience with interactive air flow
expected_outcome: Stunning first impression with breathing particles that react to mouse

decision: Keep quiz as vanilla HTML/CSS/JS (no framework migration)
reasoning: Lighter, instant load, no dependencies. Framework adds complexity without proportional benefit
expected_outcome: Fast load time, zero bundle overhead, easy maintenance

decision: Create 3 palette prototypes before final implementation
reasoning: User wants to compare Green vs Navy+Gold vs Hybrid before committing
expected_outcome: Better design decision with visual comparison, avoid rework

decision: Add comprehensive project documentation (README, CHANGELOG, CONTRIBUTING, docs/, GitHub templates)
reasoning: Project lacked documentation — no README, no changelog, no contributing guide, boilerplate admin README
expected_outcome: Clear onboarding, professional project presence, easier collaboration
